target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised €2.3M in EIC funding. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of nine private peers in the robotics sector—including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics—alongside one public comparable (277810.KQ).

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering within the narrow filters. Of the 11 same-sector peers identified, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints when filtered by stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier. Attempting to apply an all-sector median would introduce excessive methodological distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and business models. This constraint reflects the sparsity of early-stage robotics transactions with publicly disclosed valuations.

Without sufficient comparable transaction data to support triangulation, a rigorous valuation range cannot be derived from the provided dataset. Establishing defensible valuation guidance would require either: (1) expansion of the peer set to include adjacent stages or related manufacturing-tech verticals with documented terms; (2) disclosure of revenue, growth rate, or unit economics for RoboTwin that could anchor a cash-flow or revenue-multiple approach; or (3) identification of additional same-stage robotics financing rounds with known post-money valuations. The €2.3M raised provides a floor but does not establish valuation without context on dilution, pre-money terms, or the capital efficiency implied by the company's current traction.
